app.name: Understudy
app.tagline: Direct AI video with a rough take

films.title: Films
films.empty: No films yet — click “New Film” to get started
films.deleteMessage: Delete “{{name}}”? Its {{count}} shots and all extracted data will be removed. This cannot be undone.

shots.empty: No shots yet — click “New Shot” to get started
shots.noResults: No shots match the current filters
shots.tagsPlaceholder: Press Enter to add tags, e.g. night, action
shots.deleteMessage: Delete shot “{{name}}”? Its extracted data and exports will be removed. This cannot be undone.

guide.title: How it works
guide.subtitle: From rough footage to controllable AI video in five steps

create.title: Create a film & shots
create.desc: A film holds multiple shots, organized by scene and version

upload.title: Upload rough footage
upload.desc: A quick handheld take is enough — only motion and framing are used

extract.title: Extract control signals
extract.desc: Pose skeletons, depth maps and edge outlines, automatically

lens.title: Focus & cinematography
lens.desc: Click to set rack focus; pick aperture, lighting and color grade

export.title: Export & feed the AI
export.desc: One package of prompt + signals for ComfyUI or hosted platforms

board.hint: Drag edge dots to connect · double-click an edge to delete · double-click a card to open
board.dragHint: Drag cards to arrange; dragging into/out of a scene frame reassigns the shot's scene

lens.hintBar: Switch Focus/Zoom at the top of the right panel. In Focus mode, scrub to a frame and click the frame to set the focus point; in Zoom mode, click to set the center and pick a focal length. Each point shows as a diamond on the timeline, and multiple points interpolate into a rack focus / zoom.
lens.clickHintFocus: Click the frame to set focus at that point's depth (current frame)
lens.clickHintZoom: Click the frame to set the zoom center for the current frame
lens.noDepth: Focus control needs the depth channel — extract depth first
lens.noFocusKfs: Scrub to a frame, then click where you want the focus
lens.noZoomKfs: Switch to Zoom mode, click to set the center and pick a focal length
lens.zoomHint: Zoom scales every control signal (pose/depth/canny) at export and enters the prompt.
lens.promptWillInclude: Lens phrases that will enter the prompt
lens.noPhrases: None yet — enable focus or pick a focal length

export.readyDetail: Extracted {{channels}}, {{frames}} frames. Pick what to include, then package below.
export.masksAuto: Background edit masks + descriptions ({{count}} annotations, automatic)

camera.fromPreset: Inherited from the film style preset
camera.subjectPlaceholder: e.g. a young woman in a red trench coat
camera.scenePlaceholder: e.g. narrow neon-lit alley in a rainy city
camera.customPositivePlaceholder: Appended to the end of the prompt
camera.emptyPrompt: Pick parameters or fill descriptions — the prompt builds here live
camera.bgEditsHint: These are not merged into the main prompt — each region ships with its own mask and description for masked-editing tools (e.g. VACE).

provider.confirmBody: Switch to {{target}}? This reinstalls the onnxruntime dependency and restarts the app automatically (about a minute). The window will close and reopen on its own. Continue?
provider.restartingBody: The window will close while the dependency reinstalls, then reopen automatically (about a minute). Please don't kill the background process.

reset.title: Reset app
reset.hint: Erase every film, shot, control signal and export, then recreate the bundled demo projects (deletable anytime). This can't be undone.
reset.warning: All films, shots, extracted control signals, background annotations and export records will be permanently deleted, then the bundled demo projects are recreated. This cannot be undone.

preset.title: Film style preset
preset.hint: Set default cinematography for the whole film (e.g. one color grade and visual style). New shots inherit these as initial values; existing shots are unaffected.
preset.perFilm: This preset applies only to the current film — each film has its own independent style preset.

bgEdit.annotatingOn: Annotating · drag on the frame to draw
bgEdit.hint: Drag on the frame to draw boxes over background regions to remove/add/replace. Boxes export as masks for AI inpainting.
bgEdit.labelPlaceholder: e.g. Remove building on the right
bgEdit.descPlaceholder: e.g. replace with a dense forest
bgEdit.deleteMessage: Delete annotation “{{name}}” and its mask files?

preview.hintBar: Scrub the frame slider to review frames. Click “Annotate background edits” on the right, then drag on the frame to draw a box over a region to remove/add/replace; drag its corners to adjust.

upload.dropHint: Drop a video here, or click to browse
upload.replaceWarning: Re-uploading replaces the current video and clears the extracted control signals, background annotations and exports; the wizard also returns to step 1.
upload.replaceConfirm: Replace the current video? The extracted pose / depth / canny, background annotations and export history will all be removed. This cannot be undone.
upload.formats: MP4 / MOV / AVI / MKV / WebM, up to 2 GB

settings.title: Settings
settings.providerHint: CPU works everywhere; the GPU backend accelerates depth estimation (DirectML on Windows, CoreML on macOS)
settings.providerAutoNote: Switching reinstalls the dependency and restarts the app automatically — no manual steps.
settings.providerInstantNote: The switch takes effect on your next extraction — no reinstall or restart.
settings.reinstallGuide: Auto-switch is unavailable (the exe may not be built). Close the app, run this script in PowerShell from the project folder, then restart:
settings.modelsHint: Models download during install and are fetched automatically on first extraction if missing — no manual action needed.

firstRun.title: Welcome to Understudy
firstRun.subtitle: One-time setup: confirm hardware and extraction preferences
firstRun.tierCpu: No usable GPU detected — CPU inference will be used. This only affects the default processing resolution ({{size}}px) and extraction speed, not any features.
firstRun.tierLow: Less than 16 GB RAM and no usable GPU detected — using the low-spec tier. This only affects the default processing resolution ({{size}}px) and extraction speed, not any features.
firstRun.depthTitle: Depth model — Depth Anything V2 Small
firstRun.depthHint_int8: Faster and smaller, recommended for CPU
firstRun.depthHint_fp32: More accurate and detailed, slower inference
firstRun.autoDownload: Required models are downloaded during install; if missing, the first extraction fetches them automatically.
